welcome.title: Welcome
welcome.description: Answer a few quick questions so we can set your starting level. This helps match you with players of similar skill.
welcome.congratulationsSubtitle: Congratulations, your rating is set to
welcome.fillItLaterSubline: You can start this anytime from Home
welcome.q1: What is your overall experience with racket sports?
welcome.q1a: I have never played padel or any other racket sport.
welcome.q1b: I have a background in tennis/squash/badminton, but I'm brand new to padel.
welcome.q1c: I've played padel a few times and know the very basics.
welcome.q1d: I play padel regularly and feel comfortable on the court.
welcome.q2: How easily can you keep a rally going from the back of the court?
welcome.q2a: I struggle to hit the ball over the net or keep it in bounds.
welcome.q2b: I can keep a slow-paced rally going, but I struggle with my backhand or faster balls.
welcome.q2c: I am consistent on medium-paced shots, both forehand and backhand.
welcome.q2d: I can comfortably control the direction, depth, and speed of my shots during a rally.
welcome.q3: How comfortable are you using the glass/walls?
welcome.q3a: I avoid the walls completely and try to hit everything before it gets behind me.
welcome.q3b: I am starting to learn the walls and can occasionally return a simple ball off the back glass.
welcome.q3c: I am comfortable letting the ball hit the back wall and can consistently defend off it.
welcome.q3d: I use the walls to my advantage, comfortably handling double-wall bounces and fast rebounds.
welcome.q4: How confident are you when playing at the net (volleys and overheads)?
welcome.q4a: I prefer to stay at the back of the court; the net is intimidating.
welcome.q4b: I can hit basic volleys if the ball comes right to me, but I struggle with overheads.
welcome.q4c: I actively move to the net with my partner and can hit basic volleys and defensive lobs.
welcome.q4d: I am aggressive at the net and comfortable executing volleys, lobs, and basic bandejas/smashes.
welcome.q5: How would you describe your match experience and court positioning?
welcome.q5a: I'm still learning the rules, scoring, and where I should be standing.
welcome.q5b: I know the rules and basic positioning, but I just hit the ball back without much of a plan.
welcome.q5c: I understand moving up and back with my partner and try to play to the opponents' weaknesses.
welcome.q5d: I actively use strategy (like well-placed lobs and angles) to force errors and win the net.
